approach: Replace Unix-only shell syntax in codexCliProvider.ts with a uniform temp-file + shell-native stdin pipe, branching on the workspace's script setting (sh vs ps), with $ARGUMENTS substitution moved into Node. Added defensive tweaks: UTF-8 output encoding prelude on the PowerShell pipe, and CRLF→LF normalization on the temp file.
last_action: PR #123 opened — fix(ai-providers): codex cross-shell command pipe for PowerShell

concerns.note: Windows PowerShell path is not end-to-end verified (no Windows hardware). Defensive tweaks added: $OutputEncoding set to UTF-8 before the pipe, -Encoding UTF8 on Get-Content, and CRLF→LF normalization on the temp file. Residual risk: waitForShellReady was tuned for bash/zsh prompts and may need a PS regex tweak in a follow-up if users report timing issues.

decisions.note: Unified all three entry points on the temp-file pipeline — even known-skill paths go through createPromptFile now. Eliminates the dual sed/temp-file branching and keeps prompt content entirely out of the shell.
Added UTF-8 prelude and -Encoding UTF8 inside buildCodexExecCommand rather than in codexCliProvider so the builder stays the single source of truth for the ps command shape.

T001.did: Added readInitOptions(outputChannel?) + resetInitOptionsCache() with per-workspace Map cache; ENOENT stays silent, other read/parse failures log once and fall back to { script: 'sh' }.

T002.did: Added pure buildCodexExecCommand({ script, promptFilePath, permissionFlag }) emitting cat | codex exec - for sh and (UTF-8 prelude +) Get-Content -Raw -Encoding UTF8 | codex exec - for ps. Optional permission-flag insertion before the trailing stdin dash; no < redirection.

T003.did: Rewrote all three Codex entry points (executeInTerminal, executeHeadless, executeSlashCommand) to resolve $ARGUMENTS in Node, normalize CRLF → LF in createPromptFile, and stream via buildCodexExecCommand. Removed escapeForSed and the old `sed | codex exec -` and `codex exec - < file` shapes entirely.
concerns: Windows PowerShell end-to-end is unverified (no Windows hardware). Defensive tweaks added — should cover encoding and line-ending pitfalls — but a Windows user needs to confirm.

specify.key_finding: Codex provider builds shell commands in 3 call sites with Unix-only syntax (`<` input redirection and `sed` pipelines); Claude provider uses `"$(cat "…")"` substitution but the project has no shared helper for reading `.specify/init-options.json` — that infrastructure must be added.

plan.approach_summary: Replace Unix-only shell syntax in codexCliProvider.ts with a uniform temp-file + shell-native stdin pipe, branching on the workspace's script setting (sh vs ps), with $ARGUMENTS substitution moved into Node.
risks: PowerShell quoting of `Get-Content … | codex exec -`: codex exec - must accept stdin identically under PowerShell's pipeline. Mitigation: use -Raw to pass the whole file as one string (matches bash cat semantics); normalize line endings in createTempFile if CRLF becomes an issue.
`Get-Content` absent in non-PowerShell Windows shells (cmd.exe): out of scope per spec. Document the sh vs ps contract in the helper so a future cmd branch is easy to add.
